channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Vance to serve as lead US negotiator in peace talks to end Iran war, Israel and Lebanon to hold ceasefire talks, NASA's Artemis II astronauts return to Earth Friday.

item.title: Overwhelmed by kid clutter? Get organized with these 7 smart tips
item.description: If your household is starting to look like one giant playroom, it might be time to declutter. Here's how to cut down on stress-inducing mess and decide what to keep, donate or let go.

item.title: Nations tried to impose a digital fog of war in Iran. The results are mixed
item.description: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

item.title: There's growing disquiet in the military. The Iran war made it worse
item.description: Since Trump began his second term, his administration's use of the armed forces has left a growing number in the military unsettled, according to NPR interviews with service members and advocates.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts are set to return to Earth today. Here's what to expect
item.description: After swooping around the moon, viewing an eclipse, breaking an Apollo distance record and testing out a space toilet, NASA's Artemis II mission is about to return to Earth. Here's what the astronauts must face to make it safely home.

item.title: Republicans in Congress brace for a fight over the Iran war price tag
item.description: In recent days, multiple GOP lawmakers have gone public with their concerns over the war -- a range of issues Republican leadership will have to address as they face the task of securing new funding.

item.title: This week's quiz reveals a dramatic twist! OR DOES IT?
item.description: If you keep up with secret identities, you'll get at least one question right this week!

item.title: Women are getting most of the new jobs. What's going on with men?
item.description: Over the past year, the vast majority of new jobs have gone to women. One economist says to help men find work, we need to embrace ways to "make girly jobs appeal to manly men."

item.title: Communities are waiting on billions in disaster funding from the Trump administration
item.description: States say disaster funding from the Federal Emergency Management Agency has slowed to a trickle under the Trump administration. That's delaying projects to protect communities from wildfires and hurricanes.

item.title: Iran ceasefire: Not an off-ramp for the US but a life-saving ejection seat
item.description: A Middle Eastern country has for the first time single-handedly checked the war-making capabilities of the US and Israel

item.title: Djibouti votes as long-time leader seeks sixth presidential term
item.description: Politicians scrapped the presidential age limit last year, allowing Ismail Omar Guelleh to run yet again.

item.title: Schools across America are quietly admitting that screens in classrooms made students worse off and are reversing years of tech-first policies
item.description: Millions were spent putting laptops in every student's hands. Now schools want them back as technology in the classroom is turning out not to be the miracle solution it was sold as.

item.title: What Anthropic’s too-dangerous-to-release AI model means for its upcoming IPO
item.description: Anthropic says it has built its most capable AI model ever. It's also decided you can't have it.

item.title: The job market is so bad, workers now think they have worse odds of finding a role than during the pandemic
item.description: Data from the Federal Reserve Bank of New York shows that Americans are increasingly pessimistic about their job prospects.

item.title: Trump Ramps Up Iran Threats Ahead of Talks | The China Show 4/10/2026
item.description: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, David Ingles and Yvonne Man give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Russian-Flagged Tanker Transits Hormuz Into Gulf in Rare Passage
item.description: A Russian-flagged supertanker passed through the Strait of Hormuz into the Persian Gulf, marking a rare passage for one of the nation’s ships as traders scrutinize every transit through the war-hit waterway.

item.title: BMW India Head Sees Softer Luxury Car Demand on Gulf Turmoil
item.description: BMW AG’s India business is seeing affluent consumers turn more cautious as conflict in the Persian Gulf weighs on sentiment and threatens to weaken local demand for luxury cars.

item.title: Gulf Airspace Disruptions From Iran War Hits Seychelles Tourism
item.description: The number of visitors traveling to the Seychelles slumped 37% in March from a year ago, reflecting the impact of the Iran war and Middle East flight disruptions.

item.title: India Splurges on Russian Oil as Sanctions Threat Begins to Fade
item.description: Indian refiners have splurged on Russian crude in the last two months and expect to continue buying at current lofty levels for the rest of the year, as the country navigates a Middle East supply crunch and the threat of US sanctions begins to fade.

item.title: Confidence Floods Back Into US Markets: 3-Minutes MLIV
item.description: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson, Tom Mackenzie and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Guotai Junan’s Former ECM Head Is Said to Lead Share Sales After Raid
item.description: Guotai Junan International Holdings Ltd. has brought in a former senior equity capital markets banker to lead share sales after last month’s raids in Hong Kong that shook the city’s financial sector, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Abu Dhabi, Qatar Turn to Private Markets to Raise Billions
item.description: Abu Dhabi and Qatar have placed billions of dollars through private bond sales in recent weeks as the war in Iran stokes market volatility.

item.title: Warburg Pincus Starts Europe Defense Fund With Munich Re Backing
item.description: Warburg Pincus has launched a fund for investing in European defense assets amid a historic rearmament in the region that will require billions of dollars in private capital.

item.title: Taiwan Exports Surge to Record as AI Demand Outweighs Iran Woes
item.description: Taiwan’s exports jumped to an all-time high as rampant global demand for artificial intelligence chips eclipsed supply chain uncertainties unleashed by the US war in Iran.

item.title: Top-Performing Africa Fund Wagers on Urbanization to Drive Gains
item.description: A $250-million-dollar London-based equity fund is generating market-leading returns in Africa with wagers on banks and telecommunications companies.

item.title: Quadria-Backed Maxivision Eye Hospitals Said to Weigh India IPO
item.description: Quadria Capital-backed Maxivision Eye Hospitals Pvt. has begun preparations for an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, as India’s healthcare services sector continues to draw investor interest.

item.title: Giant Ballot Sheets and Obscure Candidates Baffle Peru’s Voters
item.description: On Sunday, Peruvian voters will have to squint at giant ballot sheets listing dozens of candidates they’ve barely heard of, after an attempt to streamline the nation’s electoral system fell flat.

item.title: Airbus Jet Deliveries in First Quarter Fell to Lowest Since 2009
item.description: Airbus SE delivered its fewest commercial aircraft in the first quarter since 2009, raising questions about whether the company can meet its annual target amid reverberations from the Iran war.

item.title: Fosun Weighs Sale of €2.8 Billion BCP Stake, Expresso Says
item.description: Chinese conglomerate Fosun International is weighing options for its 20.45% stake in Banco Comercial Português SA including a sale, Expresso reported, as it steps up efforts to raise cash and streamline its portfolio.

item.title: Stocks Pause With Focus on Weekend US-Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
item.description: US stocks fluctuated after a seven-day rally as investors looked to talks between the US and Iran for signs a fragile truce can hold. Oil was set for its steepest weekly loss in nine months.

item.title: 朝井辽小说《In The Megachurch》荣获日本书店大奖
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】日本2026年书店大奖的获奖作品9日揭晓，朝井辽（36岁）的小说《In The Megachurch》（日本经济新闻出版社）获奖。该奖是由日本全国的书店店员投票选出的“最想卖的书”。　　获奖小说以给偶像加油应援

item.title: 日本前国脚本田圭佑复出 加盟新加坡FC裕廊队
item.description: 【共同社新加坡4月10日电】参加过三届世界杯的日本前国脚本田圭佑（39岁）将在2026～2027赛季加盟新加坡联赛的FC裕廊队（原新加坡新潟天鹅队）。球队10日正式官宣了这一消息。本田计划8月与球队汇合。　　本田通过球队表示：“我最想实现的目标

item.title: 日印外长通电话确认霍尔木兹海峡航行安全重要性
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】日本外相茂木敏充10日与印度外长苏杰生就伊朗局势举行电话会谈。鉴于美伊11日在巴基斯坦启动旨在结束战争的谈判，日印双方一致认为包括确保霍尔木兹海峡航行安全的事态降温十分重要，并确认将继续紧密合作。　　双方还商定，将为强化能源和资源供应

item.title: 日经指数上涨1.84% 市场期待美伊谈判进展
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】东京股市日经指数10日大幅反弹，终盘报收于56924.11点，较前一交易日上涨1028.79点，涨幅为1.84%。市场期待美伊谈判取得进展及中东紧张局势缓和，买盘大量涌入。 　　东证指数（TOPIX）下跌

item.title: Зеленський: днями Україна отримала нову партію ракет до Patriot
item.description: Президент Володимир Зеленський заявив, що Україна нещодавно отримала нову партію ракет до систем протиповітряної оборони Patriot.

item.title: Британія позеленіла: чому "екологи" можуть стати головною партією країни та що це змінює
item.description: Електоральна "революція " у Британії – на перше місце вийшли "Зелені", випередивши як праворадикалів, так і "старі" партії.

item.title: Сейм Литви розгляне заборону соцмереж для дітей до 16 років
item.description: Литовський Сейм планує розглянути законодавчі поправки, які мають на меті заборонити дітям віком до 16 років користуватися соціальними мережами.

item.title: Перед виборами в Угорщині "Тиса" випереджає партію Орбана в усіх категоріях виборців
item.description: Останні опитування перед парламентськими виборами в Угорщині показують, що опозиційна партія "Тиса" Петера Мадяра випереджає політсилу "Фідес" прем’єр-міністра Віктора Орбана на 9, 11 та 13 відсоткових пунктів у різних категоріях.

item.title: Стармер каже, що втомився від впливу Трампа і Путіна на ціни на енергетику
item.description: Прем’єр-міністр Великої Британії Кір Стармер заявив, що він втомився від того, що дії американського президента Дональда Трампа та очільника Кремля Владіміра Путіна спричиняють різке зростання рахунків за енергію.

item.title: Зеленський заявив, що Україна відремонтує нафтопровід "Дружба" навесні
item.description: Президент України Володимир Зеленський повідомив, що ремонт пошкодженого російськими атаками трубопроводу "Дружба" завершиться цієї весни.

item.title: До Польщі залетіли повітряні метеозонди
item.description: До Польщі у ніч проти 10 квітня потрапили контрабанді повітряні кулі.

item.title: Португалія оголосила про наміри приєднатися до угоди про Спецтрибунал щодо агресії РФ
item.description: Португалія висловила готовність приєднатися Розширеної часткової угоди, яка дозволить запустити роботу Спеціального трибуналу з питань злочину агресії Росії проти України.

item.title: Розлив нафти спричинив перебої в роботі порту Антверпена
item.description: Судноплавство в порту бельгійського Антверпена зупинилося через розлив нафти, який поширився з одного з доків в річку Шельда.

item.title: Трамп закликав голосувати за Орбана на виборах: "Він – справжній друг"
item.description: Американський президент Дональд Трамп за два дні до парламентських виборів в Угорщині висловив підтримку прем’єр-міністру країни Віктору Орбану, а також закликав угорців голосувати за нього.

item.title: US summoned bank bosses to discuss cyber risks posed by Anthropic’s latest AI model
item.description:
Reports say Fed chair Jerome Powell among attenders at meeting in Washington

The US Treasury secretary, Scott Bessent, summoned major American bank chiefs to a meeting in Washington this week amid concerns over the cyber risks posed by Anthropic’s latest AI model, according to reports.

Bosses including the Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, were said to have gathered at the Treasury headquarters for the meeting after the release of the Claude Mythos AI model that Anthropic says poses unprecedented cybersecurity risks.

Continue reading...

item.title: Coachella 2026: Justin Bieber launches a major comeback in the desert
item.description:
Stars including Sabrina Carpenter, Karol G, David Byrne and Addison Rae also head to the desert for the first of two sold-out weekends of live music

Justin Bieber is set for a major live performance comeback at this year’s sold-out Coachella with rainy weather set to be a possible spoiler.

The Canadian singer will face his biggest live stage since he abandoned his 2022 tour over health concerns. Bieber was experiencing “full paralysis” on one side of his face after being diagnosed with Ramsay Hunt syndrome. “I wish this wasn’t the case but obviously my body is telling me I gotta slow down,” he said at the time.

Continue reading...

item.title: Starmer says he’s ‘fed up’ with Trump and Putin’s actions pushing up energy bills for Britons – UK politics live
item.description:
Prime minister explicitly blames US president for British consumers’ higher bills as he concludes tour of Gulf states

Keir Starmer has spoken to Donald Trump about the need for a “practical plan” to get shipping going through the Strait of Hormuz in the wake of the Middle East ceasefire, the Press Association reports.

Starmer spoke to Trump from Qatar, where he arrived last night ahead of talks this morning. In a readout of the call, a No 10 spokesperson said:

The prime minister spoke to President Trump from Qatar this evening.

The prime minister set out his discussions with Gulf leaders and military planners in the region on the need to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, as well as the UK’s efforts to convene partners to agree a viable plan.

The prime minister strongly condemned the Iranian attacks on Bahrain and reflected on his conversations so far with other leaders in the region.

He reiterated the importance of ensuring the ceasefire is upheld in order to pave the way for lasting peace. He said that the UK would continue to stand shoulder to shoulder with Bahrain and other allies in the region.

Continue reading...

item.title: ‘An abomination’: Lancashire town up in arms over stench from reopened landfill
item.description:
Residents of Fleetwood say continuous foul smell from Transwaste site is making life hell and making them sick

In the week that many families went to the coast for the fresh sea air or the tang of fish and chips, visitors to one Lancashire resort inhaled a rather more unpleasant aroma.

“Welcome to Fleetwood,” read the local newspaper headline. “The town that smells of bin juice.”

Continue reading...

item.title: Starmer says he is ‘fed up’ with Trump and Putin’s impact on UK energy costs
item.description:
PM appears to draw comparison between Russian and US leaders’ actions and calls for plan to restore Hormuz strait shipping

  • UK politics live – latest updates

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Keir Starmer has said he is “fed up” with the effect that Donald Trump’s actions in the Middle East are having on the British public, while appearing to draw a comparison between the US president and Vladimir Putin.

Speaking to ITV’s Robert Peston on Thursday, the prime minister said: “I’m fed up with the fact that families across the country see their bills go up and down on energy, businesses’ bills go up and down on energy because of the actions of Putin or Trump across the world.”

Continue reading...

item.title: Hungary election campaigns enter final stretch as Orbán fights to remain in power – Europe live
item.description:
Opposition candidate Péter Magyar warns supporters against complacency as some voters undecided ahead of Sunday

in Budapest

As you’re reading this Trump post, it’s probably worth keeping in mind that his VP, JD Vance, spent most of his time in Budapest fuming about the unacceptable foreign interference in the election and declaring it absolutely outrageous.

Continue reading...

item.title: Cutting fuel to Australia ‘won’t happen’, says Singapore PM, as Albanese secures pledge from our largest petrol source
item.description:
Australia and Singapore will ‘make maximum efforts to meet each other’s energy security needs’ in refined fuels and LNG, according to new agreement

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

Australia’s largest petrol source has pledged not to cut supplies, with Singapore’s prime minister telling Anthony Albanese that fuel will keep flowing despite the international crisis.

Albanese’s whistle-stop visit with his Singaporean counterpart, Lawrence Wong, culminated in a new agreement that the two countries would keep sending one another fuel and liquefied natural gas, amid the “acute energy crisis” caused by the war in the Middle East. Australia and Singapore will also add a legally binding addendum to their free trade agreement on essential supplies like energy.

Continue reading...
